examples.title: Build a small parser over a span
examples.description: A `ref struct` can safely hold `ReadOnlySpan<T>` state while it walks in-place data without allocating substrings.

examples.title: Wrap stackalloc memory in a focused helper
examples.description: `ref struct` helpers are a natural way to keep stack-only buffers together with the operations that use them.

relatedFeatures.reason: Start with ref returns when you want an earlier building block for ref struct.
Continue to Span<T> for the next guide in this theme.
Explore out / ref / in for another earlier stepping stone in the same theme.
Explore Range & index operators to take the same theme one step further.
